metadata.name: NIS2 Supply Chain Security Assessment
metadata.description: Comprehensive cybersecurity risk management and supply chain security assessment for NIS2 compliance (Articles 20-23)
metadata.scope: Essential and important entities across 18 sectors

questions.question_text: Does your management body approve and oversee the implementation of cybersecurity risk management measures in accordance with NIS2 Article 20?
questions.description: Management body must be responsible for oversight of cybersecurity risk management, including monitoring implementation and approving policies.

questions.question_text: Do members of your management body participate in regular cybersecurity training to acquire sufficient knowledge and skills per NIS2 Article 20(2)?
questions.description: Management must undergo training to gain the knowledge and skills necessary to understand and assess cybersecurity risks.

questions.question_text: Have you implemented policies on risk analysis and information system security in accordance with NIS2 Article 21(1)?
questions.description: Technical, operational, and organizational measures must be appropriate and proportionate to manage cybersecurity risks.

questions.question_text: Do you have policies and procedures for incident handling in accordance with NIS2 Article 21(2)(a)?
questions.description: Incident handling must include detection, classification, containment, response, and recovery procedures.

questions.question_text: Have you established business continuity, backup management, and disaster recovery policies including crisis management per NIS2 Article 21(2)(b)?
questions.description: Business continuity must address crisis management and include backup and disaster recovery capabilities.

questions.question_text: Are security requirements for the acquisition, development, and maintenance of network and information systems implemented per NIS2 Article 21(2)(c)?
questions.description: Security must be integrated into the full lifecycle of systems including supply chain considerations.

questions.question_text: Are network security measures including network segmentation and monitoring implemented per NIS2 Article 21(2)(d)?
questions.description: Network security must include appropriate segmentation to limit the impact of security incidents.

questions.question_text: Are multi-factor authentication or continuous authentication solutions implemented for access control per NIS2 Article 21(2)(e)?
questions.description: NIS2 explicitly requires MFA or continuous authentication solutions, along with secured voice, video, and text communications.

questions.question_text: Are secured voice, video, and text communication solutions implemented in accordance with NIS2 Article 21(2)(e)?
questions.description: Communication channels must be secured, especially for sensitive or critical communications.

questions.question_text: Do you have secured emergency communication systems operable during crises per NIS2 Article 21(2)(f)?
questions.description: Emergency communication systems must be secured and remain functional during crisis situations.

questions.question_text: Have you implemented vulnerability handling and disclosure policies and procedures per NIS2 Article 21(2)(g)?
questions.description: Processes must be in place for identifying, assessing, and remediating vulnerabilities.

questions.question_text: Are cryptographic controls including encryption implemented to protect data confidentiality and integrity per NIS2 Article 21(2)(h)?
questions.description: Encryption must be used to protect sensitive data at rest and in transit.

questions.question_text: Have you implemented security in human resources practices including access control policies per NIS2 Article 21(2)(i)?
questions.description: HR security includes background checks, security awareness training, access provisioning/deprovisioning, and acceptable use policies.

questions.question_text: Are policies for access control and asset management implemented to ensure appropriate access rights per NIS2 Article 21(2)(j)?
questions.description: Access control must follow least privilege principles with regular reviews of access rights.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct security assessments of the effectiveness of cybersecurity risk management measures per NIS2 Article 21(2)(k)?
questions.description: Regular testing must validate the effectiveness of security controls through penetration testing, vulnerability assessments, and security audits.

questions.question_text: Are cybersecurity training, education, and basic cyber hygiene practices implemented per NIS2 Article 21(2)(l)?
questions.description: All personnel must receive regular security awareness training and follow basic cyber hygiene practices.

questions.question_text: Have you established policies and procedures for the use and deployment of cryptography per NIS2 Article 21(2)(m)?
questions.description: Cryptographic policies must define approved algorithms, key management, certificate management, and usage guidelines.

questions.question_text: Are physical security and environmental security measures implemented to protect personnel and facilities per NIS2 Article 21(2)(n)?
questions.description: Physical security controls must protect data centers, network equipment, and personnel from unauthorized access and environmental threats.

questions.question_text: Do you assess cybersecurity risks related to relationships with suppliers and service providers per NIS2 Article 22?
questions.description: Supply chain risk assessment must identify and evaluate cybersecurity risks from all suppliers, including direct and indirect ICT service providers.

questions.question_text: Are appropriate security measures incorporated into contracts with direct suppliers per NIS2 Article 22?
questions.description: Contracts must include security requirements, incident notification obligations, and security assessment rights.

questions.question_text: Do you identify all direct suppliers of ICT products and services and assess their overall security level per NIS2 Article 22?
questions.description: All ICT suppliers must be identified and their security posture assessed, including compliance with security standards.

questions.question_text: Do you monitor and review the security performance of your suppliers on an ongoing basis?
questions.description: Continuous monitoring ensures suppliers maintain adequate security levels throughout the relationship.

questions.question_text: Do you have visibility into the security practices of critical subcontractors used by your direct suppliers?
questions.description: Supply chain security extends beyond direct suppliers to include critical subcontractors and fourth-party risks.

questions.question_text: Are you prepared to submit early warning notifications of significant incidents within the required timeframe per NIS2 Article 23?
questions.description: Early warning must be submitted without undue delay, and in any case within 24 hours of becoming aware of the incident.

questions.question_text: Can you submit incident notifications with required details within 72 hours of becoming aware per NIS2 Article 23?
questions.description: Incident notification must include details such as incident description, severity, impact, affected services, and initial mitigation measures.

questions.question_text: Are you prepared to submit progress reports on ongoing incidents and provide final reports per NIS2 Article 23?
questions.description: Progress reports update authorities on incident handling status. Final reports due within one month of notification include root cause analysis and mitigation measures.

questions.question_text: Do you have processes to notify authorities about significant cyber threats without undue delay per NIS2 Article 23(7)?
questions.description: Significant cyber threats (even if not resulting in incidents) must be reported to enable coordinated response.

questions.question_text: Do you notify recipients of your services when incidents significantly impact service provision per NIS2 Article 23(8)?
questions.description: Service recipients must be informed when incidents affect the provision of services to enable them to take protective measures.

questions.question_text: Are your cybersecurity measures proportionate to the risks faced, taking into account the state of the art per NIS2 Article 21(3)?
questions.description: Measures must be appropriate to the level of risk and reflect current best practices and technological capabilities.

questions.question_text: Have you identified and classified your critical ICT suppliers based on their importance to your operations?
questions.description: Critical suppliers require enhanced security oversight and may be subject to additional regulatory requirements.

questions.question_text: Do you have exit strategies and contingency plans for critical suppliers to ensure continuity of operations?
questions.description: Exit strategies mitigate concentration risk and ensure service continuity if a supplier relationship ends.

questions.question_text: Are your suppliers contractually required to notify you of security incidents that may affect your operations?
questions.description: Supplier incident notification enables timely response and mitigation of supply chain security incidents.

questions.question_text: Do you have the contractual right to audit or assess the security controls of your critical suppliers?
questions.description: Audit rights enable verification of supplier security claims and compliance with contractual requirements.

questions.question_text: Do you require suppliers of software products to follow secure development practices and provide software bills of materials (SBOMs)?
questions.description: Secure development lifecycle and transparency through SBOMs help identify and manage software supply chain risks.

questions.question_text: Is your network architecture designed with security zones and controlled interfaces between zones?
questions.description: Defense-in-depth through network segmentation limits lateral movement and contains security incidents.

questions.question_text: Do you continuously monitor network traffic for anomalies and potential security incidents using intrusion detection systems?
questions.description: Network monitoring and IDS/IPS enable early detection of attacks and anomalous behavior.

questions.question_text: Is privileged access to critical systems managed through dedicated privileged access management (PAM) solutions?
questions.description: PAM solutions provide enhanced security, monitoring, and auditing for privileged accounts.

questions.question_text: Are access rights reviewed periodically to ensure they remain appropriate and follow the principle of least privilege?
questions.description: Regular access reviews prevent privilege creep and ensure access aligns with current roles and responsibilities.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct regular vulnerability scanning of all systems and applications to identify security weaknesses?
questions.description: Automated vulnerability scanning identifies known vulnerabilities before they can be exploited.

questions.question_text: Is there a documented patch management process with defined SLAs for applying security patches based on severity?
questions.description: Timely patching of critical vulnerabilities is essential to prevent exploitation. SLAs should reflect risk.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct regular penetration testing by qualified professionals to identify exploitable vulnerabilities?
questions.description: Penetration testing simulates real-world attacks to identify vulnerabilities that automated scanning may miss.

questions.question_text: Do you regularly test and update your incident response plan through tabletop exercises or simulations?
questions.description: Testing validates the effectiveness of incident response procedures and identifies gaps before real incidents occur.

questions.question_text: Do you have documented criteria for classifying incidents by severity to enable appropriate response and reporting?
questions.description: Incident classification ensures appropriate response resources and triggers regulatory reporting when required.

questions.question_text: Do you regularly test your business continuity and disaster recovery plans to ensure they remain effective?
questions.description: Regular BCP/DR testing validates recovery procedures and identifies improvements needed to meet RTOs and RPOs.

questions.question_text: Are backup integrity and restoration capabilities verified through regular testing?
questions.description: Backup testing ensures data can be successfully restored when needed, validating both backup integrity and restore procedures.

questions.question_text: Are cryptographic keys managed securely with documented procedures for generation, storage, rotation, and destruction?
questions.description: Proper key lifecycle management is critical to maintaining the security of encrypted data and communications.

questions.question_text: Have you established a comprehensive NIS2 compliance program with assigned responsibilities and accountability?
questions.description: A structured compliance program ensures systematic implementation and maintenance of NIS2 requirements.

questions.question_text: Have you identified and established contact with the relevant national competent authority and CSIRT for your sector?
questions.description: Knowing your regulatory authority and CSIRT contact points is essential for incident reporting and regulatory coordination.

questions.question_text: Do you use a documented risk assessment methodology that identifies and evaluates cybersecurity risks to your network and information systems?
questions.description: A structured risk assessment methodology ensures consistent identification and evaluation of risks across all systems and processes.

questions.question_text: Have you developed and implemented risk treatment plans to address identified cybersecurity risks?
questions.description: Risk treatment plans document how identified risks will be mitigated, transferred, accepted, or avoided.

questions.question_text: Do you maintain a current risk register that tracks identified cybersecurity risks, their assessment, and treatment status?
questions.description: A risk register provides visibility into the organization's risk landscape and supports risk-based decision making.

questions.question_text: Are cybersecurity risk assessments reviewed and updated periodically and when significant changes occur?
questions.description: Risk assessments must be updated to reflect changes in the threat landscape, business operations, and technology environment.

questions.question_text: Have you assessed and mitigated concentration risks from reliance on single or limited suppliers for critical services?
questions.description: Concentration risk occurs when multiple critical functions depend on a single supplier, creating a single point of failure.

questions.question_text: Have you assessed geographic concentration risks in your supply chain (e.g., suppliers in single jurisdiction or region)?
questions.description: Geographic concentration can expose entities to regional disruptions, regulatory changes, or geopolitical risks.

questions.question_text: Do your supplier contracts include data protection and privacy requirements aligned with GDPR and NIS2?
questions.description: Suppliers handling personal or sensitive data must comply with GDPR and implement appropriate security measures.

questions.question_text: Do you require suppliers to maintain relevant security certifications (e.g., ISO 27001, SOC 2) appropriate to their risk level?
questions.description: Security certifications provide independent validation of supplier security controls and practices.

questions.question_text: Have you designated and communicated internal contact points responsible for incident reporting to authorities?
questions.description: Clear designation of reporting responsibilities ensures timely and coordinated incident notifications.

questions.question_text: Have you defined criteria for determining when an incident is significant and requires reporting under NIS2?
questions.description: Clear significance criteria ensure consistent determination of reporting obligations based on impact, scope, and duration.

questions.question_text: Do you have procedures for coordinating reporting when incidents affect multiple member states or cross-border services?
questions.description: Cross-border incidents may require notification to multiple authorities and coordinated response.

questions.question_text: Do you participate in voluntary cybersecurity information sharing with peer organizations and authorities?
questions.description: Information sharing enhances collective security awareness and enables coordinated defense against threats.

questions.question_text: Do you implement comprehensive security event logging across all critical systems and network infrastructure?
questions.description: Security event logs provide audit trails for incident investigation, forensics, and compliance verification.

questions.question_text: Does your organization provide cybersecurity awareness training and education programs for all personnel as required by NIS2?
questions.description: NIS2 Article 21 requires security awareness training to ensure personnel understand their role in protecting against cyber threats.

questions.question_text: Are appropriate physical security controls implemented to protect critical ICT infrastructure and data centers?
questions.description: NIS2 requires physical security measures to prevent unauthorized access to facilities housing critical systems.

questions.question_text: Is there a formal change management process for ICT systems that includes security impact analysis?
questions.description: NIS2 requires controlled change management to prevent security vulnerabilities from being introduced during system changes.

questions.question_text: Do you maintain an accurate inventory of all ICT assets including hardware, software, and data?
questions.description: NIS2 requires comprehensive asset management to understand what needs to be protected and monitor for unauthorized changes.

questions.question_text: Are security events and logs collected, protected, and regularly reviewed for security incidents?
questions.description: NIS2 requires comprehensive logging and monitoring to detect and respond to security incidents promptly.

questions.question_text: Do you assess and manage cybersecurity risks from third-party service providers and suppliers?
questions.description: NIS2 explicitly requires managing supply chain cybersecurity risks, including vendor assessments and contractual security requirements.

questions.question_text: Do you follow secure software development lifecycle (SDLC) practices including security testing and code reviews?
questions.description: NIS2 requires security to be integrated into the development process to prevent vulnerabilities in custom applications.

questions.question_text: Do you regularly test and exercise your incident response capabilities through simulations and drills?
questions.description: NIS2 requires organizations to validate their incident response readiness through regular testing and exercises.

questions.question_text: Are appropriate technical and organizational measures in place to protect personal and sensitive data in accordance with GDPR and NIS2?
questions.description: NIS2 requires data protection measures that complement GDPR requirements, ensuring confidentiality and integrity of data.
